questions.question: Johannes Hoesflot Klaebo, who won a gold medal in cross-country skiing at the Milan–Cortina Winter Olympics 2026, belongs to which country?
questions.explanation: Norway skier Johannes Hoesflot Klaebo won his ninth gold medal in cross country skiing at Milan Cortina Winter Olympics 2026. He set a new Winter Games record with this achievement. The 29 year old secured his fourth gold medal of the 2026 Games. He anchored Norway team in the men 4 into 7.5 kilometre relay event. Earlier he was sharing the record with three retired Norwegian athletes. With this win, he became the most successful cross country skier in Winter Olympics history.

questions.question: Which Indian State/UT has created history by conducting the world’s highest frozen lake half marathon?
questions.explanation: Ladakh conducted its first 21-km trail run at Pangong Tso, held at 13,862 feet in sub-zero temperature, and it was recorded in Guinness World Records as the world’s highest frozen lake half marathon. Pangong Tso is an endorheic, landlocked lake located in eastern Ladakh and extends into China’s Tibet region. The name means “high grassland lake” in Tibetan. It lies at about 4,350 meters and is one of the world’s highest brackish water lakes. The lake changes colours from blue to red and green.

questions.question: Which country was officially awarded the 2025 Africa Cup of Nations title?
questions.explanation: Morocco national football team have been awarded the Africa Cup of Nations 2025 title after the Confederation of African Football overturned the original final result. The decision followed a ruling that stripped Senegal of the title, granting Morocco a 3-0 administrative victory after Senegal walked off the pitch in protest during the final. The Confederation of African Football (CAF) overturned the result of the final match following a review of a walk-off protest by Senegal’s players, which occurred in stoppage time. Morocco were crowned champions, winning their first major tournament title on home soil, while Senegal was officially listed as forfeiting the final.

questions.question: Which country topped the medal table at the World Athletics Indoor Championships 2026?
questions.explanation: The World Athletics Indoor Championships 2026 ended with record-breaking performances and highest quality in history. United States topped the medal table with 5 gold, 7 silver, 6 bronze (total tally 324). Event recorded highest performance score of 49,516, surpassing 2022 record. Future editions will be held in India (2028) and Kazakhstan (2030).

questions.question: Who has been named ‘Para Archer of the Year 2025’ by World Archery?
questions.explanation: Sheetal Devi has been named ‘Para Archer of the Year 2025’ by World Archery. She is a 19-year-old para archer from Jammu and Kashmir, known for competing without arms, making her achievements exceptional. She became the first and only female armless archer to win gold at the World Para Archery Championships. She won gold in the women’s compound individual event at Gwangju, South Korea. She also won a bronze medal at the Paris Paralympics in the mixed team event.

questions.question: Who won 1 gold and 2 silver medals at the Spanish Para Badminton International 2026 – Level 1?
questions.explanation: Pramod Bhagat won 1 gold and 2 silver medals at the Spanish Para Badminton International 2026 – Level 1. He secured gold in mixed doubles with Manisha Ramdass. He won silver in men’s singles after losing to Nitesh Kumar. Another silver came in men’s doubles with Sukant Kadam.

questions.question: Which Indian player has been declared as the outstanding Men’s Player of the Year 2025 by the Asian Squash Federation (ASF)?
questions.explanation: Asian Squash Federation named Abhay Singh as Men’s Player of the Year 2025. Anahat Singh won the top honour in the girls (junior) category. The Indian boys team won the Men’s Team Award after securing bronze at the World Junior Team Championships 2025. Previously, Saurav Ghosal and Joshna Chinappa won ASF awards in 2022.

questions.question: Which state topped the medal tally in Khelo India Tribal Games 2026?
questions.explanation: The Khelo India Tribal Games concluded in Chhattisgarh, with the closing ceremony attended by Chief Minister Vishnu Deo Sai, highlighting the promotion of tribal sports talent. Renowned athletes Mary Kom and Bhaichung Bhutia participated as special guests, encouraging young players. In the overall medal tally, Karnataka emerged as the champion with 23 gold medals, followed by Odisha (second) and Jharkhand (third). The host state Chhattisgarh finished ninth with 3 gold, 10 silver, and 6 bronze medals. A total of about 3,800 athletes from 30 States and Union Territories participated, reflecting wide national representation.

questions.question: Rassie van der Dussen, who has announced his retirement from international cricket, belongs to which country?
questions.explanation: South African player Rassie van der Dussen has announced his retirement from international cricket at the age of 35, ending a successful career as a dependable white-ball batter for South Africa national cricket team. He debuted late at 29 in a T20I against Zimbabwe and went on to play 18 Tests, 71 ODIs, and 57 T20Is. His strongest format was ODIs, where he scored 2657 runs at an average above 50, making him second only to AB de Villiers among South Africans. He was a key player during the 2019 Cricket World Cup and became a regular middle-order batter.

questions.question: Palak and Mukesh Nelavalli won which medal in the 10m air pistol mixed team event at the ISSF World Cup 2026?
questions.explanation: Palak and Mukesh Nelavalli won gold medal in the 10m air pistol mixed team event at the ISSF World Cup 2026, Spain. They set a new world record with a combined score of 487.7, also marking a junior world record. The previous record was held by Uzbekistan shooters. In the final, they defeated China (silver) and Hungary (bronze). International Shooting Sport Federation (ISSF) governs the event.
